title: Strange Supreme
content:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Strange Supreme}} {{SomethingElse|the [[Earth-838]] [[Variant]]|Supreme Strange|Supreme Strange}} {{Otheruses|Doctor Strange}} {{CharHub|Strange Supreme|Stephen Strange}} {{AltUniverse|the "[[Corrupted Doctor Strange Universe]]"|3=Characters|4=1}} {{Character |image = Supreme Strange profile pic.png |name = Strange Supreme |real name = Stephen {{ConjecNameAU|Vincent}} StrangeThis character's middle name is derived from a Variant [[:File:Doctor Strange Bone & Muscle Scan.jpg|Stephen Strange's Surgery Files]] and thus is conjecturally treated as the same.{{Ref|WI104}} |alias = {{Alias |codenames = Doctor Strange{{Ref|WI104}}
Strange Supreme{{Ref|WI104}}{{Ref|WI209}} |nicknames = {{WPS|Armani}}{{Ref|WI104}} |undercover = [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]]{{Ref|WI104}}
[[Hydra Stomper|Steve Rogers]]{{Ref|WI209}}}} |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA}} |gender = Male |title = Doctor
[[:Category:Sorcerer Supreme|Sorcerer Supreme]] |affiliation = {{Affiliation|MGH}} (formerly)
{{Affiliation|MMACDS}} (formerly)
[[Guardians of the Multiverse]] (formerly) |tv series = {{Infobox List|''[[What If...?]]'' (7 episodes)
{{Collapse|''[[She-Hulk: Attorney at Law]]'' (photo)}}}} |docuseries = ''[[Legends]]''
*''[[Guardians of the Multiverse (episode)|Guardians of the Multiverse]]'' |game = {{Infobox List|{{Collapse|''[[What If...? – An Immersive Story]]'' (depiction)}}}} |voice actor = [[Benedict Cumberbatch]]
[[Rachel McAdams]] (disguised)
[[Josh Keaton]] (disguised) |status = Deceased}} {{Quote|How many nights did we sit in the [[Sanctum Sanctorum]], [[Time Stone]] in the palm of our hand, knowing we could go back, gathering the courage? Well, I had the courage. I spent [[Before 20th Century/Corrupted Doctor Strange|centuries]], sacrificed everything for [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine]].|Strange Supreme to [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|Doctor Strange]]|What If... Doctor Strange Lost His Heart Instead of His Hands?}} Doctor '''Stephen {{ConjecNameAU|Vincent}} Strange''' M.D., Ph.D. was the Sorcerer Supreme and a [[Masters of the Mystic Arts/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Master of the Mystic Arts]]. Strange became hellbent on resurrecting his late girlfriend [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]], after she died in a car accident, which ultimately resulted in the destruction of his [[Corrupted Doctor Strange Universe|entire universe]]. Floating for a time in a parcel of land protected by a bubble of the [[Mirror Dimension]]'s magic, Strange, now known as '''Strange Supreme''', was later recruited by [[Watcher]] to help him save the [[Multiverse]] from [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]]. Strange, alongside [[Captain Carter/Super Soldier Peggy Carter|Captain Carter]], [[Thor/Party Prince Thor|Thor]], [[Erik Killmonger/Killmonger's War|Erik Killmonger]], [[Gamora/Sakaarian Iron Man|Gamora]] and [[T'Challa/Ravager T'Challa|Star-Lord]], were brought together and formed the [[Guardians of the Multiverse]]. Once Ultron was erased by [[Arnim Zola/Age of Ultron|Arnim Zola]], both Zola and the traitorous Killmonger tried to gain the [[Infinity Stones]], leading to Strange trapping them in a shrunken time bubble. Strange was then instructed by the Watcher to watch over the prisoners, a role Strange took gladly. However, Strange's isolation caused him to once again go mad with grief, deciding to capture universe killers and various heroes and sacrifice them all to his [[Forge]] to resurrect his dead universe. His plans were foiled by [[Captain Carter/Super Soldier Peggy Carter|Captain Carter]] and [[Kahhori]], leading Strange to regain his senses and sacrifice himself and his demons instead to restore his universe and Christine. However, this cost him his life and his existence in the [[Reforged Universe|reborn universe]], though Strange's soul continued to exist, coursing through every atom of his new universe, which he continued to shape. ==Biography== {{MD|Stephen Strange (Earth-91233)}} ===Changing the Universe=== ====[[Stephen Strange's Car Crash/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Demise of Christine Palmer]]==== [[File:What If 1x4 4K screenshot2.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange collects [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]] for a date]] {{Quote|We need to celebrate this."
"We are."
"You performed a radical hemispherectomy. Successfully."
"Well, that was the plan.| [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]] and Stephen Strange|What If... Doctor Strange Lost His Heart Instead of His Hands?}} One night, Stephen Strange arrived at [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]]'s apartment to pick her up for a night out at a dinner party. When Palmer exited the building, calling Strange handsome, Strange replied that he just pulled his suit out of the dryer, though he followed up by charismatically calling her stunning. Palmer told Strange that she was coming along just for the crème brûlée, with Strange remarking that they could do better than that. [[File:What If 1x4 4K screenshot3.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange and [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]] drive to a party]] On the way to a dinner while driving on a cliffside road, Palmer asked Strange to tell her the speech he had prepared. Strange attempts its opening, though Palmer immediately found it boring, suggesting to get to the heart of it or rather wing it like a contestant from ''{{WPS|The Price Is Right}}'', a show Strange was unfamiliar with. Palmer reminds Strange that he had performed a radical hemispherectomy successfully, which Strange affirmed was part of the plan. [[File:Strange-Sees-Christine-Die.PNG|{{AltFloat}}|Strange loses [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]]]] Palmer added that it was a remarkable one, with Strange commenting that she herself was remarkable, making Palmer chuckle. While attempting to pass a truck, Strange was immediately forced back into his lane by an oncoming vehicle, rattling Palmer. Strange attempts to affirm to Palmer that she was safe, though their car would be rear-ended by another speeding car, forcing them off the side of the cliff and into a river bank below. While Strange was relatively unharmed from the crash, Palmer had ultimately perished, much to a heartbroken Strange's despair. [[File:MastersOfMysticArts - WhatIfEp4.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Strange training with the [[Masters of the Mystic Arts/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Masters of the Mystic Arts]]]] Grief-stricken, Strange traveled the world searching for answers to give him hope. He found the [[Ancient One/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Ancient One]] and the [[Masters of the Mystic Arts/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Masters of the Mystic Arts]]. He eventually was trained by the Sorcerer Supreme and came across the [[Eye of Agamotto]]. While testing it on an apple, he considered using it to [[time travel]], only to be warned by the Ancient One and [[Wong/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Wong]] that tinkering with time would threaten the fabric of the universe. Following the Ancient One's death, Doctor Strange saved [[Earth]] from [[Dormammu/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Dormammu]]'s rule and became the new Sorcerer Supreme.{{Ref|WI104}} ====Traveling Back in Time==== [[File:What If 1x4 4K screenshot5.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange at the [[New York Sanctum]]]] {{Quote|Stephen. Something wrong?"
"Why does this keep happening? Aren't we allowed to be happy?|[[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]] and Doctor Strange |What If... Doctor Strange Lost His Heart Instead of His Hands?}} On the two year anniversary of [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]]'s death, Strange couldn't help but think of the past, remembering an evening kiss he shared with her as she wore his t-shirt while he sat alone in front of the [[New York Sanctum]]'s window. [[File:What If 1x4 4K screenshot4.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange listening to [[Wong/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Wong]]]] [[Wong/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Wong]] appeared and stood beside him, noticing Strange's attempt to hide the [[Eye of Agamotto]] while he reminisced of Palmer. Wong advised he join him while he prepared a kettle before he did anything reckless. Once Wong had left the room, Strange stared at the Eye before deciding to use it to transport himself back in time to the moment he picked up Palmer from her apartment. [[File:Strange Time Stone What If.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Strange prepares to [[Time Travel|travel back in time]]]] From there, events play out relatively the same, with Strange prematurely mentioning things Palmer would have told him such as what to do when he gave his speech. However, despite not attempting to pass the truck this time, his car still was rear-ended by the speeding car, resulting in Palmer's death again. Desperate, Strange used the Eye again to constantly repeat events from before, vainly trying to prevent Palmer's death. Despite his efforts, such as taking an alternate route to the dinner or abandoning her, Palmer would always perish in some way. [[File:Strange-Ancient-One-Talk.PNG|{{AltFloat}}|Strange and the [[Ancient One/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Ancient One]] talk]] Emerging from his wrecked car broken and defeated one last time, Strange could only yell in frustration, pounding the ground in anger. Strange eventually was greeted by the [[Ancient One/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Ancient One]], who informed Strange that Palmer's death was unchangeable, an Absolute Point; without it, Strange would never have joined the [[Masters of the Mystic Arts/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Masters of the Mystic Arts]] and eventually rise to defeat [[Dormammu/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Dormammu]]. [[File:W104-0004785.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange ignores the [[Ancient One/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Ancient One]]'s warnings]] Despite this, Strange was unable to accept her words, arguing that she taught him that nothing was impossible and that he only required more power. When told by the Ancient One that not even the greatest sorcerers of the past could bypass an Absolute Point, Strange resolved to going back in time to acquire knowledge lost to time. The Ancient One attempted to dissuade Strange, but he continuously refused. [[File:Strange-Is-Split.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange being [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|split into two people]]]] While Strange prepared the Eye of Agamotto, the Ancient One interfered and drew power from the [[Dark Dimension]] to split Strange's mind and body into two, with one half traveling centuries into the past and the [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|second half]] traveling the destined path he was meant to tread. Both halves of Strange would be oblivious to the other's existence for the time being, simultaneously co-existing in the same timeline and universe.{{Ref|WI104}} ====Seeking Knowledge==== [[File:Strange-Meets-O'Bengh.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange meets [[O'Bengh]]]] {{Quote|You have to be the strangest-dressed sorcerer I've ever seen"
"Uh... Yeah, it's {{WPS|Armani}}.|[[O'Bengh]] and Doctor Strange |What If... Doctor Strange Lost His Heart Instead of His Hands?}} Doctor Strange arrived centuries back in time to the middle of a jungle, where he is greeted by an unknown man. Strange attempted to ask the man if he knew where the [[Lost Library of Cagliostro]] was, though he gave no answer and walked away. Strange followed him into a clearing, where he was greeted by the sight of the lost library. [[File:What If...? 28.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange is surrounded by runes]] {{Rewrite|tense}} Seeing that there was no door, Strange opened a [[Inter-Dimensional Portal|portal]] to the library's facade where he discovered a protection rune on the ground. Strange dispelled the rune with his magic and walked further in, only to be surrounded by numerous other runes and plunged into a dark abyss. Upon regaining his senses, Strange is greeted by the man from before, who comments on Strange's unusual attire for a sorcerer, which Strange attempts to justify by saying it was {{WPS|Armani}}. [[File:Strange - W104 00048.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange is approached by [[O'Bengh]]]] Strange inquires if he knew where [[Cagliostro/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Cagliostro]] was, though the man cryptically dodges the question. Strange asks if he himself was Cagliostro, though the man reveals himself to be [[O'Bengh]], the librarian for the books of Cagliostro. O'Bengh tells Strange to follow him, calling him "Sorcerer Armani," though Doctor Strange vainly attempts to clarify that his name was Strange. [[File:What If...? 36.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange seeks knowledge]] O'Bengh led Strange to the Lost Library of Cagliostro, inquiring how long he'd stay, with Strange responding with however long it took. Using his powers, Strange navigated through the vast collection of books until he found one regarding time manipulation. Reading through the book, Strange discovered that the power to break an Absolute Point in time is nigh impossible to obtain but can still be achieved by gaining power through the absorption of other beings.{{Ref|WI104}} ====Gaining Power==== [[File:W104 - 1987854.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange summons a [[Champion of Hydra|tentacled creature]]]] {{Quote|Is [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|she]] worth the pain? A man does not suffer like this for his own glory."
"Every moment of it.|[[O'Bengh]] and Doctor Strange|What If... Doctor Strange Lost His Heart Instead of His Hands?}} Isolating himself in a secluded room, Strange used the knowledge from the book to manifest a portal to summon other dimensional beings, the first one being a [[Champion of Hydra|tentacled creature]]. Strange attempted to bargain with the being to borrow its power, only for the creature to grab Strange by his leg and fling him around the room until he was knocked unconscious. [[File:W104 - 2145879.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange is questioned by [[O'Bengh]]]] Strange awoke to [[O'Bengh]] nursing him, who told him that dimensional beings do not bargain to share their power that man cannot wield. O'Bengh asked if [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]] was worth the pain he was inflicting to himself, with Strange stating that it was worth every moment. Before leaving, O'Bengh told Strange that there was a fine line between devotion and delusion and the means in which love is achieved, and when Strange asked where he heard that from, O'Bengh simply responded that life taught him that. [[File:Strange-Absorbs-Gnome.PNG|{{AltFloat}}|Strange absorbs his first [[Gnomes|mystical being]]]] Once healthy again, Strange returned to his library, where he came to the conclusion that he must forcefully take other dimensional beings' powers. Upon freezing himself in time, Strange started small by summoning a [[Gnomes|gnome]], which Strange promptly absorbed. Following the gnome, Strange summoned a mystical bug with a black magic cloak pretty similar to the [[Cloak of Levitation]], which Strange took for himself before de-summoning the bug, remarking that he drew the line at bugs. [[File:Watcher's presence is noted by Strange.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange detects [[Watcher]]'s presence]] From there, Strange steadily continued absorbing more and more stronger beings, briefly taking on their appearances himself as he absorbed them. While Strange caught his breath after absorbing a winged bat-like being, [[Watcher]] decided against the notion to interfere, as this was the predetermined path Strange had chosen; Strange however could sense the Watcher's presence observing him and asked who was there, though he would receive no answer. [[File:Strange-and-the-HYDRA-Champion.PNG|{{AltFloat}}|Strange faces the [[Champion of Hydra|tentacled creature]] again]] As centuries passed, Strange absorbed countless various powerful mystical beings. Eventually, Strange once again came face to face with the tentacled creature he first encountered, and using his amped-up powers, managed to sever the beast's tentacles and absorb them into him. With that, Strange unfroze himself from time, and soon encountered an old and dying O'Bengh, who revealed Strange had been draining the power of mystical entities for centuries. [[File:Strange-Sees-O'Bengh-Die.PNG|{{AltFloat}}|Strange reunites with a dying [[O'Bengh]]]] Strange attempted to heal O'Bengh and make him live forever with the [[Eye of Agamotto]], though he was stopped by O'Bengh, who told Strange that death was part of life, which Strange admitted he cannot accept. O'Bengh revealed that while he couldn't accept, the other [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|Doctor Strange]] could, much to Strange's confusion. Before dying, O'Bengh told Strange that he was only half a man living half a life.{{Ref|WI104}} ====[[Duel at the End of the Universe|Confronting His Other Half]]==== [[File:Strange-Meets-Himself.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange meets [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|Doctor Strange]]]] {{Quote|Where am I?"
"The question is, where are you? Do not be frightened, old friend, for we are one and the same.|[[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|Doctor Strange]] and Doctor Strange|What If... Doctor Strange Lost His Heart Instead of His Hands?}} As reality started to break down due to Strange's presence after he unfroze himself from time, he soon lured the other [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|Doctor Strange]] to the [[Lost Library of Cagliostro]] and trapped him in an imprisonment spell. There, Strange revealed himself to the other Strange, who questioned Strange on what he had become. [[File:W104 - 369875.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange talks about [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]]'s death]] Ignoring the question, Strange lamented to his other half that only they knew [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]], though the other Strange interjected, saying that they couldn't save her without the world's destruction, much to Strange's anger, who let out a burst of dark energy showing the silhouettes of the various beings he had absorbed. [[File:What If 1x4 4K screenshot13.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange debating with [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|Doctor Strange]]]] Regaining himself, Strange told his other self that he had sacrificed everything to save Palmer, taking centuries of his life to achieve his goal. The other Strange asks if he could now save her, though Strange denied, saying they can before grabbing the other Strange and teleporting away with him. [[File:What If...? 46.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange prepares to fight [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|Doctor Strange]]]] Strange brought himself and the other Strange back in time to the crash site of their car, where he revealed that once they were united as one, he would finally have enough power to bring back Palmer. The other Strange tried to dissuade Strange from doing this, though he refused to listen, resorting to trying to absorb the other Strange on the spot, though he was impeded by the other Strange's protection spell placed on him by [[Wong/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Wong]]. Strange then engaged in a duel with his other half, retorting that he had gone too far to back down. Strange then starts charging the other Strange, summoning multiple giant serpent-like creatures from the ground to attack him. Over the course of the battle, Strange slowly chipped away at the other Strange's protection spell, and even destroyed the other Strange's [[Cloak of Levitation]]. [[File:Strange turning into Palmer.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange disguised as [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]]]] Eventually, the other Strange managed to send a powerful blast of energy towards Strange, seemingly destroying him. While the other Strange was distracted by a forming pure light from where Strange stood, Strange snuck up from behind in the other Strange's shadow and dragged him into an illusion. Within the illusion, Strange disguised himself as Palmer to trick the other Strange, although he didn't believe it was her and Strange broke the illusion, sending the two freefalling from the sky. [[File:Strange is absorbed by Corrupted Strange.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange absorbs his [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|other half]]]] During the fall, the two Stranges traded blows, but ultimately Strange emerged victorious after completely destroying the other Strange's protection spell. Despite the other Strange's protests, Strange absorbed his weakened other half, finally making him whole again.{{Ref|WI104}} ====The End of the Universe==== [[File:Strange resurrects Palmer.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange resurrects [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]]]] {{Quote|You were warned."
"I know, but the world! The world shouldn't pay for my arrogance. I-I read about you, sensed your presence. You're a god. You can undo this."
"I'm not a god. And neither are you.|[[Watcher]] and Doctor Strange|What If... Doctor Strange Lost His Heart Instead of His Hands?}} With his full potential achieved, Strange combined his power with the power of the [[Time Stone]] and released a giant burst of [[Dark Dimension]] energy. After the explosion, Strange's Time Stone turned dark red and he transformed into a composite form of all mystical creatures he has absorbed, he then immediately used his newfound power to break the Absolute Point in time and finally resurrect [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]]. [[File:WI104 - 00047847.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange talking to [[Watcher]]]] However, this action was the breaking point for the universe's stability as reality quickly fell apart around them. As Strange struggled to keep himself and Palmer alive from the universe's collapse, he witnessed [[Watcher]]'s presence and vainly begged him to help, willing to do anything to save the universe. The Watcher refused, however, stating that any more interference with space and time would be disastrous, condemning Strange to his fate. [[File:Christine - reality's destruction.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange sees [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]] die]] The universe was soon destroyed in its entirety, save a small patch of land held together by Strange's magic. As Palmer faded away in his arms questioning what he had done, Strange sobbed and apologized, left alone as the last survivor of his home universe.{{Ref|WI104}} {{Clear}} ===Saving the Multiverse=== ====Aiding Watcher==== [[File:Doctor Strange Supreme in What If... Ultron Won?.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Strange's help is requested by [[Watcher]]]] {{Quote|Been living the dream alone in a prison of my own making ever since. Are you ready to break your oath?"
"You want me to say it?"
"Oh, yeah. Ohhh, yeah. I wanna hear you say it."
"Okay. I can't believe I'm about to say this. I see now. I need your help.|Doctor Strange and [[Watcher]]|What If... Ultron Won?}} Later, Doctor Strange was visited by [[Watcher]] physically in what was left of his universe, the [[Mirror Dimension]]. There, the Watcher solemnly told Doctor Strange that he was out of options to combat [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]], who was from a different universe, after Ultron had breached the boundaries of the [[Multiverse]] and taken control of the [[Observational Plane]]. Doctor Strange sympathized with the Watcher, and asked if he was ready to break his oath aloud. Though initially hesitant, the Watcher mustered enough courage to directly ask Doctor Strange for help.{{Ref|WI108}} ====Guardian of the Multiverse==== [[File:Watcher and the Guardians of the Multiverse.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange reunites with his [[Guardians of the Multiverse|teammates]]]] Doctor Strange constructed a bar in a dimension outside of space and time and waited there as [[Watcher]] gathered a team together. Those chosen were then transported to the bar and Doctor Strange was introduced to [[Captain Carter/Super Soldier Peggy Carter|Peggy Carter]], [[T'Challa/Ravager T'Challa|T'Challa]], [[Erik Killmonger/Killmonger's War|Erik Killmonger]], [[Thor/Party Prince Thor|Thor]], and [[Gamora/Sakaarian Iron Man|Gamora]]. Strange explained the [[Infinity Stones]] to them, while The Watcher explained the [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|threat]] they were up against. These five, along with Strange were then dubbed the [[Guardians of the Multiverse]] by The Watcher and were transported to an uninhabited planet on a different universe. [[File:Doctor Strange-Protection Spell4.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Strange gives protection spells to his [[Guardians of the Multiverse|teammates]]]] Once on the other universe, Strange tried to control his dark magic powers and was asked by Carter what had happened to him. He replied the same happened to him that happened to her which was love. He then told her about his universe's [[Steve Rogers/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Steve Rogers]] which made Carter interested. Strange then conjured drinks for the rest of them and began to make a toast. However, during his toast, he started rambling and was stopped by T'Challa who gave a proper toast instead. In his excitement, Thor, accidentally alerted their presence to [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]] who saw them, much to Strange's frustration as the team wasn't yet ready for Ultron's arrival. Ultron then appeared out of a [[Wormhole]], and Strange distributed out protection spells as further protection, much to the team's fascination. After Thor blasted [[Mjølnir]] at Ultron, Strange used his spells to make copies of Mjølnir and sent them at Ultron. Ultron managed to defeat this spell and soon realized that he was missing an [[Soul Stone|Infinity Stone]]. In order to buy the team time to leave through another [[Inter-Dimensional Portal|portal]], Strange summoned the [[dragons]] he had absorbed to attack Ultron. [[File:Doctor Strange Supreme and Party Thor.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange watches the [[Zombies|zombies]] attack [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]]]] However, Ultron destroyed the dragons, forcing Strange to summon a horde of [[zombies]] from another universe in order to stall Ultron. He used a [[Inter-Dimensional Portal]] to bring the zombies, specifically the zombified [[Scarlet Witch/Zombie Outbreak|Wanda Maximoff]] there, and dropped them on top of Ultron. He then hopped into the [[Wormhole|portal]] and arrived in Ultron's home universe. [[File:Strange absorb a galaxy-sized explosion.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Strange absorbing a galaxy-sized explosion]] After returning to Ultron's universe, Strange and the other Guardians met [[Black Widow/Age of Ultron|Natasha Romanoff]] who was from that universe. They then were confronted by Ultron after he arrived and Strange used his [[Sling Ring]] to protect Romanoff as well as giving her a protection spell. He and the Guardians then battled with him in order to reclaim the [[Soul Stone]]. However, an exasperated Ultron unleashed the Stones' full power in an attempt to obliterate his universe completely, but to his surprise, Strange used the Time Stone to reverse the explosion and then absorbed the blast, leaving Ultron confused and dumbfounded. [[File:Strange unleashing his power.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange transforming]] Strange then attacked [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]] continuously with his teammates but Ultron activated his Time Stone to create a time dome, freezing everyone inside. Strange quickly turned his arm into a tentacle to incapacitate Ultron and used his own Time Stone to dissolve the dome. Ultron grew angry and used the Infinity Stones' power to enlarge himself. In response, Strange unleashed part of his full power, transforming into his mixed composite form of mystic beings and launched dozens of giant tentacles towards Ultron, neutralizing him and smashing him to the ground. [[File:Strange Supreme and Thor.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Strange hearing [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]]'s voice]] Strange then instantly restrained Ultron using binding spells with the help of other Guardians in order for [[Gamora/Sakaarian Iron Man|Gamora]] to use her [[Infinity Crusher]] on Ultron's [[Infinity Stones]]. Due to the Infinity Stones being from a different universe, it resulted in an explosion. Ultron survived the explosion and teleported above them, proclaiming that each universe was different, and the Stones were unique therefore the Infinity Crusher has no effect on him. Ultron realized Strange was the reason why he could not kill the others and focused solely on killing Strange. Strange endured long enough for Carter and Romanoff to fire a USB arrow into Ultron's eye, downloading [[Arnim Zola/Age of Ultron|Arnim Zola]]'s analog consciousness into his system and successfully killing him. [[File:Strange Supreme's Pocket Dimension.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Strange trapping [[Erik Killmonger/Killmonger's War|Killmonger]] and [[Arnim Zola/Age of Ultron|Zola]] in a pocket dimension]] When [[Erik Killmonger/Killmonger's War|Erik Killmonger]] betrayed the team and battled with Zola now in Ultron's body for the Infinity Stones, Strange realized their purpose was never meant to win but to separate the Stones from Ultron, with the help from [[Watcher]], Strange used the [[Mirror Dimension]] to create a pocket dimension to trap both Zola and Killmonger inside.{{Ref|WI109}} ====A New Post==== [[File:Strange with The Watcher.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange agrees to watch the pocket dimension]] {{Quote|You foresaw every moment from the failed [[Infinity Crusher]], to [[Erik Killmonger/Killmonger's War|Killmonger]]'s betrayal and my..."
"Your sacrifice."
"Sacrifice? That would imply I had something to lose.|Doctor Strange and [[Watcher]]|What If... The Watcher Broke His Oath?}} Afterwards, Strange transported himself back to his universe inside the [[Mirror Dimension]] and spoke to [[Watcher]] about how he had foreseen everything. The Watcher then asked Strange if he could protect the pocket dimension from breaking in which Strange agreed, stating that he had nothing but time and that is what friends were for.{{Ref|WI109}} ===Restoring His Universe=== ====Descent into Madness==== {{Quote|Strange isn't trying to save universes. He's trying to resurrect his [[Corrupted Doctor Strange Universe|universe]]. The one he destroyed.|[[Kahhori]] to [[Captain Carter/Super Soldier Peggy Carter|Captain Carter]]|What If... Strange Supreme Intervened?}} Over time, Strange was driven mad from the grief over destroying his own universe, so he decided to restore it, with the main intention of bringing back [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]].{{Ref|WI209}} using knowledge given to him by Watcher.{{Ref|WI308}} To do so, Strange created the [[Sanctum Infinitum]] as his base of operations, and inside he built the [[Forge]], a machine capable of restoring his universe. To achieve this goal, Strange devised a plan to capture various beings across the [[Multiverse]], including universe killers and righteous heroes, and sacrifice them into the Forge. Strange traveled across universes and imprisoned countless beings.{{Ref|WI209}} ====Finding Kahhori==== [[File:Doctor Strange meets Kahhori.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange arrives in [[Kahhori]]'s universe]] {{Quote|Bravo. World peace in record time. I'm sure that'll last. But I digress. My name is Stephen Strange, and I've been looking for you for a long time.|Doctor Strange to [[Kahhori]]|What If... Kahhori Reshaped the World?}} Strange discovered the existence of [[Kahhori]], a powerful hero from another universe who he could feed to the [[Forge]]. Strange opened a portal to her universe, and he greeted Kahhori, who was brokering a peace treaty with Queen [[Isabella I of Spain]]. Strange sarcastically congratulated Kahhori on achieving world peace and told her that he had been searching for her for a while.{{Ref|WI206}} Kahhori decided to follow him back to the [[Sanctum Infinitum]], however, once she learned Strange's true intentions, she fled to another universe by going through the [[Rotunda of Gateways]].{{Ref|WI209}} ====Reunion with Captain Carter==== [[File:Peggy Carter and Doctor Strange.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange is reunited with [[Captain Carter/Super Soldier Peggy Carter|Captain Carter]]]] {{Quote|You're collecting universe killers."
"Yeah, well, someone's gotta do it. And we both know the [[Watcher]] would never stoop so low as to intervene.|[[Captain Carter/Super Soldier Peggy Carter|Captain Carter]] and Doctor Strange|What If... Strange Supreme Intervened?}} Knowing that it would be now harder to capture [[Kahhori]], Strange decided to enlist the help of [[Captain Carter/Super Soldier Peggy Carter|Captain Carter]], who was currently trapped in a different [[Forerunner Steve Rogers Universe|universe]] from her own. Strange found Carter in a bar and sat down with her. Carter was relieved to see Strange and the two joked with each other, before Carter asked to be taken back to her [[Super Soldier Peggy Carter Universe|universe]]. [[File:Peggy Carter & Doctor Strange.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange enlists the help of [[Captain Carter/Super Soldier Peggy Carter|Captain Carter]]]] Strange brought Carter to the [[Sanctum Infinitum]] and told her that since they last met he had been protecting the [[Multiverse]] by capturing universe killers. Seeing that Carter believed his deception, Strange told Carter that he needed her help. Strange explained that one of his prisoners escaped and he needed her help. Carter was hesitant, thinking about what the [[Watcher]] would say about intervening, but Strange persuaded her into helping. Strange told Carter that she would know the escapee when she saw her and sent her off, promising to take her home afterwards.{{Ref|WI209}} ====[[Battle at the Sanctum Infinitum]]==== [[File:Sinister Doctor Strange Supreme.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange attempts to capture [[Kahhori]]]] {{Quote|For what it's worth, I really thought you would have understood. I gave you a choice, Peggy Carter. They will feed my [[Forge|machine]]. I must resurrect my [[Corrupted Doctor Strange Universe|world]]. And you, my friend, are finished.|Doctor Strange to [[Captain Carter/Super Soldier Peggy Carter|Captain Carter]]|What If... Kahhori Reshaped the World?}} Once [[Captain Carter/Super Soldier Peggy Carter|Captain Carter]] found [[Kahhori]], Strange opened a portal and brought them both back to the [[Sanctum Infinitum]]. Strange immediately attacked Kahhori to keep her from escaping again, but she revealed to Carter what Strange was really doing. Carter attempted to make Strange stop what he was doing, but he was determined to save his universe. [[File:What If... S2 Strange Supreme.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Strange attempts to contain his prisoners]] Strange claimed that Carter would do the same for [[Hydra Stomper|Steve Rogers]] in hopes that she would side with him, but Carter protected Kahhori. Strange continued to attack Kahhori, so Carter, unable to stop Strange, threw her [[Captain Carter's Shield|shield]] at the orbs containing his prisoners, freeing them all. With a riot breaking out, Strange stopped attacking Kahhori and turned his attention to containing everyone. Strange started the [[Forge]] and prepared to feed his prisoners into it. [[File:Images of Ikkon (What If).png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange creates many duplicates of himself]] Strange was confronted by Kahhori and Carter, the latter who was now wearing the [[Infinity Armor]], allowing her to wield the [[Infinity Stones]], although Strange was not worried. Carter and Kahhori worked together to fight Strange and destroy the Forge, but he was able to counter their attacks. Kahhori told Strange that there were more of them than him, so he decided to drastically outnumber them by creating duplicates of himself using the [[Images of Ikonn]]. Carter did the same using the [[Reality Stone]], but Strange soon turned her copies into butterflies. [[File:Steven Strange.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange as [[Hydra Stomper|Steve Rogers]] in an illusion]] In an attempt to end the fight, Strange cast an illusion making Carter think she was at [[Project Rebirth]]. Pretending to be Rogers, Strange tried to convince Carter that she could have what she wanted, but she was able to break out of the illusion. Strange opened several portals above the Forge and his prisoners began to fall through. Kahhori was able to save the prisoners, so Strange attacked her, causing them to fall back down. After disabling the effects of Carter's [[Time Stone]] with his own, Strange kept Carter and Kahhori from stopping him further. [[File:Captain Carter vs. Strange Supreme.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange fighting against [[Captain Carter/Super Soldier Peggy Carter|Captain Carter]]]] With his prisoners falling towards the Forge, Strange told Carter that he had hoped she understood but due to her standing in his way, he must end her life. However, several of the prisoners gave Carter their weapons to strengthen her, and she was able to blast Strange away. Kahhori sent all of the prisoners back to their universes, but Strange refused to accept defeat and he went to fight, despite knowing Carter and Kahhori were a match for him. As they fought, Strange transformed into a demon consisting of his absorbed beings. [[File:Fighting your inner demons.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange sacrifices himself to destroy his demon]] Now completely taken over by the demon, Strange overpowered all of Carter and Kahhori's attacks. Carter held the Infinity Stones and used their power to punch Strange in the face, forcing the demon out of his body and temporarily restoring Strange's sanity. With the Forge about to explode, Carter asked Strange if they could stop it; he told her he could not as his grief was too strong and had grown beyond Strange's control. The demon returned to Strange and tackled Carter into the Forge. Carter managed to save herself, and Strange partially emerged and restrained the demon from attacking her. As they both fell, Strange accepted his fate, and the Forge consumed him and the demon.{{Ref|WI209}} ===Legacy=== ====A New Universe==== {{Quote|Strange's plan worked."
"Yes, but at the cost of his life. He recreated [[Reforged Universe|this universe]], but was never born into it and never will be."
"To think of all the suffering he caused for a world he would never get to see.|[[Captain Carter/Super Soldier Peggy Carter|Captain Carter]] and [[Watcher]]|What If... Kahhori Reshaped the World?}} As a result of his sacrifice to the [[Forge]], Strange's universe was successfully recreated. He would never be born into it, though his soul nevertheless continued to exist there, coursing through every atom in it, shaping the destinies of its inhabitants.{{Ref|WI308}} As [[Captain Carter/Super Soldier Peggy Carter|Captain Carter]] and [[Watcher]] watched [[Christine Palmer/Reforged Universe|Christine Palmer]] in the [[Reforged Universe|new universe]], they discussed the suffering that Strange had caused only to never get to see his world returned. They also noted that despite the demons inside of him, Strange was able to redeem himself in his final moment by defeating his demons and saving his world at the cost of his own life. Watcher and Carter continued to recognize Strange as their friend and mourned his loss.{{Ref|WI209}} One of the things that Watcher was put on trial for was sharing with Strange the information that he needed to recreate his universe. Watcher defended himself by pointing out that Strange had sacrificed his own soul to resurrect the home that he destroyed. Watcher called it a miracle that continued to birth new branches of reality for the Watchers to watch over.{{Ref|WI308}} ====Helping His Friends==== [[File:Strange Stars.png|{{AltFloat}}|Strange helps out his [[Guardians of the Multiverse|friends]] one final time]] {{Quote|What? Why aren't my powers working?"
"Because you're in his world now."
"Stephen Strange is..."
"A [[Reforged Universe|living universe]]. And in here, he has absolute power. You have none.|[[Eminence]] and [[Watcher]]|What If... What If?}} While battling [[Incarnate]], [[Eminence]] and [[Executioner (Watcher)|Executioner]], Watcher and Carter decided to take the fight to Strange's new universe. The two explained to the new [[Guardians of the Multiverse]] that Strange's soul coursed through every atom of his new universe which was shaped by him. The two hoped that Strange was still perceptive enough to help them by restricting the Watchers' powers. After Carter sacrificed herself to transport her friends to that universe, Incarnate, Eminence and Executioner were rendered powerless as hoped. An image of Strange appeared amongst the stars, looking out for his old friend one more time. Watcher explained to Eminence that Strange had become a living universe, one in which he had absolute power.{{Ref|WI308}} ==Powers and Abilities== === Powers === * '''Universal Manipulation''': After sacrificing his body to the [[Forge]], Strange created a universe where he was never born but in which he nonetheless lives on as his soul now courses through every atom of it. He is able to influence and shape everything within his universe, even foreign elements present within. **'''Power Nullification''': Strange is able to nullify the powers of any beings present within his universe, including those of multiversal foreigners present within, as he demonstrated by cancelling the [[Eminence]]'s powers. ===Former Powers=== {{Quote|Without her death, you would never have defeated [[Dormammu/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Dormammu]] and become the Sorcerer Supreme, and the guardian of the [[Eye of Agamotto]].|[[Ancient One/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Ancient One]]|What If... Doctor Strange Lost His Heart Instead of His Hands?}} *'''[[Magic]]''': Having become the Sorcerer Supreme, Doctor Strange possessed an extremely high level of mastery over magic, which he used to defend his native reality. With the death of the Ancient One, Doctor Strange is currently the most powerful member of the [[Masters of the Mystic Arts/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Master of the Mystic Arts]]. After absorbing countless mystical beings over the centuries, Strange gained near unfathomable level of arcane power. He attained cosmic awareness, allowing him to sense, see, and communicate with [[Watcher]]. After reaching his full potential, Strange gained the power to break an Absolute Point in time which the [[Ancient One/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Ancient One]] referred as an "unchangeable and unmovable" event, which indirectly destroyed his universe. After the destruction of his universe, Strange was the only one remain due to his power. Even the nigh-omnipotent [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]] acknowledged Doctor Strange's powers, as he was completely shocked and surprised at him being able to absorb a galaxy-sized explosion of energy with no ill effect, as well as saying that the [[Guardians of the Multiverse]] were able to stand a chance against him only because of Strange and Strange was the only one unaffected by the time dome created by Ultron's Time Stone. This indicates that Doctor Strange is the most powerful member of the Guardians of the Multiverse. Even with the Infinity Stones, [[Kahhori]]'s powers and the magical weapons and items of several powerful beings, Captain Carter and Kahhori struggled to fight him. **'''[[Magic#Eldritch Magic 2|Eldritch Magic]]''': Strange is able to shape and manipulate Eldritch Magic, forming tangible constructs of energy, such as weapons or shields, as well as cast spells by writing specific formations with the fiery energy. ***'''Spell Casting''': [[File:Doctor Strange-Protection Spell2.jpg|thumb|250px|Strange casting protection spells]]Doctor Strange can cast spells by writing specific formations with the Eldritch Magic's fiery energy. He was able to stop an Eldritch Magic energy blast and used Dark Dimension energy to transform it into a binding spell to restrain his [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|other half]]. He was even able to restrain [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]] using the binding spell by binding both his hands and legs in conjuncture with Dark Dimension energy blasts using the powers from all of the mystical beings that he absorbed, and the help of [[Captain Carter/Super Soldier Peggy Carter|Captain Carter]], [[Erik Killmonger/Killmonger's War|Killmonger]] and [[Thor/Party Prince Thor|Thor]] by pulling the chains. They were able to hold him long enough for [[Gamora/Sakaarian Iron Man|Gamora]] to attach the [[Infinity Crusher]] onto the [[Infinity Stones]] in effort to destroy them. Using an imprisonment spell, Strange was able to trap an alternate version of himself. His alternate self was not able to use his magic within the barriers of the spell. When [[Thor/Party Prince Thor|Thor]] used [[Mjølnir]] against Ultron, Strange was able to cast a spell on the hammer to multiply it into numerous hammers that behaved exactly like the original. Strange was also able to cast Vishanti Protection Spells to protect the [[Guardians of the Multiverse]] by giving them an extra layer of armor as well as shields made of magic and even incorporates their weapons. He was also able to cast several protection spells around all of them within a limited area. Indeed, these shields and armors were so immensely strong that were able to withstand the combined might of an alternate Ultron, using either some or all the Infinity Stones for a considerable amount of time including a galaxy-sized explosion of energy, and being able to withstand the energy released from five of the Infinity Stones when [[Arnim Zola/Age of Ultron|Zola]] and Killmonger were dueling over the control of them. Strange was also able to recast the protection spell once some or all of its runes have dissipated, as he has shown to cast it again on Captain Carter and [[Black Widow/Age of Ultron|Black Widow]] after their protection spell had completely dissipated. Besides protecting the users, Vishanti Protection Spell also enhanced all their physical attributes to incredible superhuman levels, numerous times greater than they already were. It enhanced their physical attributes so much that even a normal human like Black Widow could stagger and knock down Ultron. The protection spell also incorporated the weapons that they used, including Thor's [[Mjølnir]], Gamora's [[Double-Edged Sword]], Captain Carter's [[Captain America's Shield|shield]], T'Challa's [[Quad Blasters]], Erik Killmonger's Vibranium Claws and Black Widow's [[Red Guardian's Shield]], enhancing their offensive, defensive capabilities and durability tremendously, likely hundreds of times greater than they already were. Their enhanced weapons were strong enough to even stagger and hurt Ultron, as well as successfully block Ultron's attack even when he was using the power of multiple Infinity Stones. Also, while explaining the Infinity Stones to the Guardians of the Multiverse, Strange generated a holographic display of the cosmos and the stones by conjuring Eldritch Magic formations. ***'''Portal Creation''': Strange is able to cast portals without the need of a [[Sling Ring]] thanks to his level of mastery. During his training he has mastered this power much better than his [[Doctor Strange|original self]] and later he opened a portal to get inside the [[Lost Library of Cagliostro]], due to the fact that there were no doors. Furthermore, Strange is able to open portals to other timelines and universes in the Multiverse, displayed when he dragged his [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|alternate self]] from the separated timeline created by the Ancient One to his place or opened a massive portal to summon countless zombies from a different universe on Ultron. ****'''Dimensional Travel''': Strange is able to travel outside space and time as well as anywhere in the Multiverse shown when he searched the Multiverse for a universe where Ultron wouldn't notice him and the [[Guardians of the Multiverse|Guardians]]. ***'''Energy Projection''': [[File:What If...? 43.png|thumb|250px|Strange holds a [[Mirror Dimension]] barrier]] Strange can project energy bolts, blasts and beams that are amplified by [[Dark Dimension]] energy. During which he used in battle against his [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|other half]]. During this fight, he hurled spheres of energy that homed in on his counterpart but were blocked by his shields, sent arcs of red energy surging through his counterpart's magical bindings in order to free himself and repel the other Strange and generated energy from his arms to burn and tear the [[Cloak of Levitation]] off when it attempted to restrain him. He notably was able to hold a [[Mirror Dimension]] barrier against the collapsing darkness using his beams. When using the powers from all of the mystical beings that he absorbed, the energy blasts were potent enough to even cause [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]] to experience pain. Later, Strange used his energy blasts to attack and contain Ultron during the final battle with the AI. **'''[[Magic#Dark Dimension Magic 2|Dark Dimension Magic]]''': [[File:Strange-Dark-Magic.png|thumb|250px|Strange's corrupt magic]] Strange is able to manipulate Dark Dimension Magic after spending his time at the [[Lost Library of Cagliostro]], using it to summon mystical beings, absorb their lifeforce and use their powers. He is also able to amplify his basic powers by using the energy from the Dark Dimension. By transforming parts or all of his body into his composite mystical being form, Strange can resist the manipulation of other [[Time Stone]] over him even if its from another universe as seen when he was able to move freely inside Ultron's time sphere. ***'''Immortality''': By drawing power from the Dark Dimension, Strange was able to prolong his lifespan far beyond regular humans, and with his [[Time Stone]] he used magic to remain frozen for centuries in order for him to absorb as many mystical beings as possible. With the powers of all the mystical beings that he had absorbed, he became immortal, which he stated that he would watch over the pocket dimension containing Killmonger and Zola as he had nothing but time. ***'''Conjuration''': [[File:Strange-Summons-Bird-Demon.PNG|thumb|250px|Strange conjures a mystical being]] Using spells imbued with the energy of the Dark Dimension within the [[Book of Cagliostro]], Doctor Strange was able to contact and summon mystical beings from other dimensions onto a runic circle he constructed. Additionally, he was able to locate and send his [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|other half]] falling through a portal and onto his circle to confront him. During their battle, Strange was able to summon massive, canine-like serpents that burrowed seamlessly through the ground towards his alternate self, who destroyed them with blasts of energy. He was later able to summon a three headed dragon and the [[Champion of Hydra]] to battle an alternate Infinity Stones-infused Ultron. Strange also make a self-refilling mug of beer appear for [[Thor/Party Prince Thor|Thor]] and conjured Chinese food as well as a tray loaded several different drinks for the Guardians of the Multiverse. ***'''Shapeshifting''': [[File:Monstrosity Supreme Strange.jpg|thumb|250px|Strange's monstrous form]] After absorbing countless mystical beings, Strange was able to shift into each of them right after absorbing them. For example, he was able to manifest a [[Champion of Hydra|tentacled being]] in his hand to grab his [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|alternate self]] and create ripples of large serpents. He was also able to shift into a mesh of all those creatures, either at will or by accident. Additionally, Strange was able to hide himself within his alternate self's shadow before grabbing him from behind as a mass of darkness and drag him into a Mirror Dimension illusion space in a final attempt to convince him to join him willingly. After he absorbed his [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|other half]], he shifted into a mesh of all those creatures when he used the powers from all of the mystical beings that he had absorbed and combined with the power of the Time Stone in order to resurrect Palmer. Later, he once again shifted into this form when he used the powers from all of the mystical beings that he absorbed, creating Dark Dimension energy blasts and binding spells to attack Ultron. ***'''Cosmic Awareness''': Due to his absorption of various mystic beings, Strange eventually became powerful enough to sense [[Watcher]]'s presence, and later even see and communicate with him. ***'''Life-Force Absorption''': [[File:Strange-Absorbs-Red-Dragon.png|thumb|250px|Strange absorbing a mystical being]] After learning how to summon beings from other dimensions, Strange was able to absorb their lifeforce within his spirit, enhancing his powers. However, this came at a cost when he sometimes transformed into an amalgamation of the beings he consumed. He was even able to absorb his [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|alternate self]] after he removed the protection spell from him. ***'''Energy Absorption''': Strange can absorb different types of energy and later using it for different purposes, like enhancing his own powers. During the battle between [[Guardians of the Multiverse]] and [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]], Strange was even able to absorb a galaxy-sized explosion of energy released by Ultron using the power of five [[Infinity Stones]], Strange consumed the energy which made his eyes glow like fire and increased his power. Later, Strange used this energy to counteract the power of all Infinity Stones released by Ultron. This could only temporarily counteract as the energy released by Ultron was too strong, while Strange was unharmed, his teammates were unavoidably blown away. **'''[[Magic#Mirror Dimension Magic 2|Mirror Dimension Magic]]''': Following his training at [[Kamar-Taj]] as well as the [[Lost Library of Cagliostro]], Strange is able to manipulate [[Mirror Dimension]] Magic. Strange was able to maintain a Mirror Dimension barrier for a short time until the darkness collapsed upon him. However, he was able to maintain a Mirror Dimension bubble, which is the only thing left in his universe. ***'''Illusion Manipulation''': Strange demonstrated being able to create an illusory pocket space capable of simulating [[Metro-General Hospital]] as well as disguise himself as Christine Palmer to trick his alternate self. ***'''Dimension Creation''': Strange was able to trap Erik Killmonger and the Arnim Zola-possessed Ultron body inside a smaller pocket dimension bubble with the help of the Watcher as they were duelling over five of the alternate Ultron's universe's Infinity Stones and contain the resulting energy unleashed by them within the bubble. **'''Time Manipulation''': [[File:What If...? 20.png|thumb|250px|Strange using the [[Time Stone]]]] With the [[Eye of Agamotto]], Strange is capable of controlling and manipulating time on immeasurable level, including rewinding, and freezing time for centuries and even break Absolute Point in time, a nigh-impossible thing to achieve. Using it, Strange rewound time to moments before [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]]'s death, hoping that he could find a way to stop her from dying, and later traveled centuries into the past to find the [[Lost Library of Cagliostro]]. He notably used this ability to trap even [[Dormammu/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Dormammu]] himself in the same moment, which would reset each time he killed Strange, keeping Dormammu from leaving his dimension to invade Earth until he agreed to leave Earth for good. Strange used this ability on himself to remain frozen in time for centuries to absorb mystic creatures but not aged even a second. Later Strange wanted to use it on [[Cagliostro/Corrupted Doctor Strange|O'Bengh]] to make him immortal but O'Bengh refused. He was also able to use his [[Time Stone]] to counteract and break the effects of an alternate [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]]'s own Time Stone on the [[Guardians of the Multiverse]]. ***'''Resurrection''': After absorbing his other [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|half]] and achieving his full potential, Strange was able to finally break the Absolute Point in time and fully resurrect Palmer. **'''Telekinesis''': Strange is able to influence objects with mere gestures, as seen during his search inside the [[Lost Library of Cagliostro]]. By extending his hands, he caused several books to launch from their shelves, drift around the large chamber and present themselves to him before setting themselves down when he found the book he was looking for. He also was able to turn the pages of one of the books he was reading with a wave of his hand. After summoning a bug entity, he proceeded to suspend it a few feet in the air, cause its cloak to fly onto himself and drop it back through the magical circle it came from. In the process of resurrecting her, Strange was able to cause an unconscious Palmer and the car she died in, the latter of which disassembled, to gently levitate out of the crash site and over to his side. During the first battle against [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]], after having cast a duplication spell on Mjølnir, Strange was able to telekinetically manipulate the multiple hammers to trap Ultron. **'''Levitation''': Using his alternate version of the [[Cloak of Levitation]] stolen from a mystical bug, Strange is able to levitate, hover, and fly, although with all of his powers, he may not need it to fly. **'''Transmutation''': ''To be added'' ===Abilities=== {{Quote|You're a genius.|Christine Palmer|What If... Doctor Strange Lost His Heart Instead of His Hands?}} *'''Genius-Level Intellect''': Strange's level of intellect allowed him to achieve his MD and Ph.D. simultaneously at medical school, going on to become one of the world's most renowned neurosurgeons. With this level of intellect, he was able to master multiple types of magic manipulation and use their powers to his benefit. When Zola and Killmonger dueling over the control of the Infinity Stones, Strange was able to immediately realize that the Guardians of the Multiverse were never meant to win, but rather to permanently separate the Infinity Stones from Ultron. Strange's photographic memory allows him to recall all of the moments before [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]]'s death even after two years since the event. Using this, he was able to use the [[Eye of Agamotto]] to go back to those moments multiple times. Even after going insane from grief, Strange was able to come up with a way to restore his destroyed universe which was successful despite only Strange himself and his grief falling into his Forge. *'''Master Physician''': Strange is a highly accomplished doctor. According to Palmer, he successfully performed a radical hemispherectomy, which she called "quite remarkable". *'''Expert Martial Artist''': [[File:What If...? 48.png|thumb|250px|Strange fighting against his [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|alternate self]]]] Following his studies at [[Kamar-Taj]], Strange became proficient in martial arts, both armed and hand-to-hand combat. *'''Bilingualism''': Strange is fluent in his native English, and became proficient enough with Sanskrit during his months in [[Kamar-Taj]] to enable him to study advanced books such as the [[Book of Cagliostro]]. ==Equipment== ===Other Equipment=== *'''[[Eye of Agamotto]]''': Strange used this ancient relic with a number of mystical properties, especially time manipulation in the user's immediate vicinity and on specific areas or targets. It is able to do so since it contains the [[Time Stone]]. *'''[[Cloak of Levitation]]''': Strange's Cloak of Levitation is different from the original, having a different design as well as formerly being used by a mystical bug. It appears to be sentient and can move on its own power, causing it to move and flap in the air without the presence of wind. Offensively, the cloak's ability to move and fly with or without a wearer allows it to grab, lift, tackle or send enemies flying through the air. The cloak assisted Strange in defending him against an [[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|alternate version]] of him and his [[Cloak of Levitation#Strange Supreme|cloak]]. *'''[[Book of Cagliostro]]''': The Book of Cagliostro contains many powerful spells that are imbued with dimensional energies, such as the [[Dark Dimension]]. Using this book, Strange was able to summon mystical beings and absorb their lifeforce, strengthening him and his powers. ===Vehicles=== *'''{{WPS|Lamborghini Huracán}}''': Strange used to own and drive a Lamborghini Huracán, however, while he was driving with [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]] to one of his speaking events, another car hit them from behind and Strange lost control, completely throwing them off a hill as a result, destroying the car, and killing Palmer during the impact. ==Facilities== *'''[[Metro-General Hospital]]''': Prior to Palmer's death, Strange worked as a successful neurosurgeon at the Metro-General Hospital alongside her. *'''[[Kamar-Taj]]''': Strange studied magic at Kamar-Taj, eventually overseeing it as Sorcerer Supreme. *'''[[New York Sanctum]]''': Strange mainly resided in the New York Sanctum prior to the universe's destruction. *'''[[Lost Library of Cagliostro]]''': In order to find a way to reverse [[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]]'s death, Strange went back in time to find [[Cagliostro/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Cagliostro]]. However, he ended up meeting [[O'Bengh]], who guided him into the library. There, Strange spent his time studying the dark arts and strengthening his powers for centuries. *'''[[Sanctum Infinitum]]''': Strange created the Sanctum Infinitum, where he held countless [[:Category:Sanctum Infinitum Inmates|multiversal beings]] prisoner. He intended to use the souls of the prisoners to power a [[Forge]] that, once completely powered, would revive his [[Corrupted Doctor Strange Universe|home universe]]. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Allies=== *[[Christine Palmer/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Palmer]] † - Girlfriend and Accidental Victim *[[Nic West/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Nicodemus West]] † - Accidental Victim *[[O'Bengh]] † *[[Watcher|Uatu/Watcher]] - Former Situational Enemy and Friend *[[Guardians of the Multiverse]] - Former Teammates and Friends **[[Captain Carter/Super Soldier Peggy Carter|Peggy Carter/Captain Carter]] † - Former Attempted Victim **[[T'Challa/Ravager T'Challa|T'Challa/Star-Lord]] **[[Thor/Party Prince Thor|Thor]] **[[Gamora/Sakaarian Iron Man|Gamora]] **[[Black Widow/Age of Ultron|Natasha Romanoff/Black Widow]] *[[Shadow Snakes]] {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Dormammu/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Dormammu]] † - Accidental Victim *[[Masters of the Mystic Arts/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Masters of the Mystic Arts]] - Former Allies **[[Doctor Strange/Temporal Paradox|Stephen Strange/Doctor Strange]] † - Alternate Self **[[Ancient One/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Ancient One]] † - Former Mentor and Former Friend **[[Wong/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Wong]] † - Former Friend and Accidental Victim *[[Christine Everhart/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Christine Everhart]] † - Accidental Victim *[[Steve Rogers/Corrupted Doctor Strange|Steve Rogers/Captain America]] † - Accidental Victim *Mystical Beings **[[Gnomes]] **[[Dragons]] ***[[Great Protector/Multiversal Prisoner Great Protector|Great Protector]] - Former Prisoner and Attempted Victim *[[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]] † - Attempted Killer, Attempted Victim and Former Prisoner *[[Erik Killmonger/Killmonger's War|N'Jadaka/Erik Killmonger]] - Former Teammate, Former Prisoner and Attempted Victim *[[Arnim Zola/Age of Ultron|Arnim Zola]] - Former Prisoner and Attempted Victim *[[Demons]] *[[Peter Quill/Celestial Invader Ego|Peter Quill]] - Former Prisoner and Attempted Victim *[[Loki/Gunslinger Loki|Loki]] - Former Prisoner and Attempted Victim *[[Star-Lord/Multiversal Prisoner Star-Lord|Peter Quill/Star-Lord]] - Former Prisoner and Attempted Victim *[[Freak/Gamma Enhanced Happy Hogan|Happy Hogan/Freak]] - Former Prisoner and Attempted Victim *[[Vision/Multiversal Prisoner Vision|Vision]] - Former Prisoner and Attempted Victim *[[Captain Marvel/Multiversal Prisoner Captain Marvel|Carol Danvers/Captain Marvel]] - Former Prisoner and Attempted Victim *[[Thor/Mad Viking Thor|Thor]] - Former Prisoner and Attempted Victim *[[Einherjar/Einherjar General Thor|Einherjar]] - Former Prisoners and Attempted Victims **[[Thor/Einherjar General Thor|Thor]] -
